body: Don't do what? Could you elaborate?
don't convert sass to scss, at least until I haven't finished to work on it plz.
Yep, sure, this is a work in progress. We'll not merge it until you're finished don't worry :)
I like the idea (but I feel for @Shipow :D)

I've rebased on 1.0.0. Tests are failing because...
![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/283419/11239635/817cf266-8ded-11e5-888b-fd870afbf06f.png)
I was thinking waiting a few days, until the release is over, and then fix all the linting issues
@pixelastic do you want to move forward with this one?

I would like us to have some Travis testing on SCSS for the website, so we cannot push things that will break.

But this means fixing all the SCSS lint issues, which can be quite long. I'm not sure I'll have time to do it in the next two weeks, but I can do it afterwards.
Ok do you agree on closing this one and reopen it when relevant?
@pixelastic can you copy the output of this scss warnings ?

@vvo: Yep, we can close this one

@Shipow: Here is the output. It uses the same rules as the one we are using in the main project. Some are easy to solve (removing units on `0` values). Some are harder (do not overspecify selectors) because we must make sure the rendering stay the same.

